title: sciodaphyllum digynum
intro: Sciodaphyllum digynum, also known as the False Hoya or Tukas, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of the Philippines. Traditionally, Sciodaphyllum digynum is used in folk medicine, where its leaves are valued for their medicinal properties, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. Sciodaphyllum digynum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, or even used in traditional remedies such as topical applications or poultices.
